raw_text: ABAYOMI-COLE, A. B.—b. 1907; ed. Govt. Model Sch., Prince of Wales Sch., S.L., and Durham Univ.; M.O., S.L., 1944.

ABBOTT, A. W., C.B.E. (1949).—b. 1893; ed. Blundell's; entd. C.A. off. after compet. exam., 1912; mil. serv., 1917–19; lieut.; asst. hd., fin. dept., 1929; dep. hd., 1933; hd., 1938; sec., E.A. currency bd., 1930–38; estab. offr., 1948; sec., C.As., 1954.

ABBOTT, M. J.—b. 1906; ed. King's Sch., Canterbury; barrister, Gray's Inn; mil. serv., 1941–45; lieut.; asst. crown solr., H.K., 1936; secon. to Ethiopian Govt. as pres., high ct., 1946; puisne judge, Nig., 1950; judge, 1953.

ABBOTT, S. H.—b. 1908; ed. Antigua Gram. Sch.; appt. agric. dept., Antigua, 1929; agric. asst., St. Kitts, 1939; farm manager, 1944; agric. offr., Virgin Is., 1946; Nevis, 1949; agric. supt., Antigua, 1955.
ABELL, Sir Anthony, K.C.M.G. (1952), C.M.G.—b. 1906; ed. Repton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; cadet, Nig., 1929; admin. offr. cl. II, 1946; cl. I, 1948; gov. and c.-in-c., Sarawak, 1950.

ABERNETHY, J. S.—b. 1907; ed. Sedbergh Sch., Aberdeen Gram. Sch., Aberdeen and Edin. Univs.; interned, 1942–45; chartered co., N. Borneo; legal advr., comsnr. of lands, prot. of labr. (incl. inspr., schs.), 1936; food contrlr. (in addn.), 1941; sessions judge, B.M.A. (B. Borneo), 1946; comsnr., lands, 1947; sessions judge, 1948; res. mag., Tang., 1949; judge of high ct., 1951.

ABRAHAM, A. A.—b. 1912; ed. St. Stanislaus Coll., B. Guiana; cler. asst., B. Guiana, 1929; dist. offr., 1945; estab. offr., 1947; prin. asst. sec., 1953; ch. estab. offr., 1955, mem. publ. serv. comsn.
ABRAHAM, A. L., M.V.O. (1954), C.P.M.—b. 1908; ed. Southend High Sch.; police, Pal., 1930; supt., Barb., 1938; Fiji, 1944; B. Hond., 1950; comsnr., Gib., 1953.
ABRAHAMS, A. R.—b. 1908; ed. J'ca Coll.; apptd. J'ca, 1927; pensions offr., 1944; asst. sec., 1950; prin., 1952; perm. sec., min. of commns. and wks., 1954.

ABRAHAMS, H. A.—b. 1906; ed. Calabar High Sch., J'ca; asst., constab., J'ca., 1923; 2nd cl. clk., constab., 1933; 1st cl. clk., med. dept., 1939; examr., accts., audit dept., 1944; acctnt., dept. of agric., 1948; senr. acctnt., 1950; admin. sec., med. dept., 1952; man., govt. savings bank, 1955.

ABRAHAMS, Rt. Hon. Sir Sidney, P.C. (1941), Kt. Bach. (1936), Q.C.—b. 1885; ed. Bedford Modern Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; called to Bar, Middle Temp., June, 1909; hon. Bencher, 1948; Midland circuit; town mag., Zanz., 1915; cr. coun., Ken., 1915 (did not proceed); secon. Mespot. civ. admin., 1920; advoc.-gen. and registr. of companies, Baghdad, 1920; pres., ct. of first instance, ct. of sessions and ct. of criminal appl., Basrah, 1921; atty.-gen., Zanz., 1922; Uga., 1925; G.C., 1928; ch. just., Uga., 1933; Tang., 1934; Ceylon, 1936–39; member of jud. comtee. of privy cl., 1941; jt. edtr., Zanz. law rept., 1868–1918; sole edtr., 1918–22; 2nd cls. Order of Brilliant Star of Zanz., 1924; addl. ast. leg. advr., C.O., 1939; sole comsnr. to enquire into Nyasaland land problems, 1946; edited (with A. Abrahams) Dagens Nyheter Stadian Edn., 1912; various contributions to journalism on track athletics.

ACOLATSE, C. S.—b. 1899; ed. Livingstone Coll., Salisbury, U.S.A., and Cambridge Univ.; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; dist. mag., G.C., 1943; puisne judge, 1952; mem., comsnr. enq., strike and riots, Freetown, S.L., 1955.
ADAMES, P.—b. 1913; ed. Cranleigh Sch. and Oriel Coll., Oxford, Camb. Univ. and I.C.T.A.; agric. offr., S.L., 1940; prin. agric. offr., 1951.
ADAMS, A. B.—b. 1899; ed. Wolmer's Sch., J'ca, London and Illinois Univs.; senr. master, tech. sch., San Fernando, Trin., 1954; prin., 1955
ADAMS, H. L.—b. 1910; ed. Haileybury; mil. serv., 1939-45, lt.-col.; cadet, Ken., 1946; sec., comrce. and industry, 1949; econ. sec., E.A.H.C., 1954.

ADAMS, P. F., O.B.E., Medal for Merit (U.S.A.)—b. 1914; ed. Mitcham Secondary Sch. and Lond. Univ.; I.C.S., 1937-47; col. admin. serv., sec. to devel. comsnr., Tang., 1948; prin. ast. sec. (devel.), 1950; M.C.S., cl. IC, ag. contrlr., econ. division, 1953; mem., jt. working party, rubber industry, 1953-54; leader, Mal. reps., inter. rubber study group mtg., Colombo, 1954.
ADAMS, R. K.—b. 1911; asst. registr., co-op. socs., S.L., 1939; Nig., 1946; S.L., 1950.
ADAMSON, G. A. G., M.B.E.—b. 1906; mil. serv., 1940-41; game ranger, Ken., 1939; senr., 1955.

ADDIS, Sir Wm., K.B.E. (1955), C.M.G. (1948), Brill. Star (3rd cl.), Zanz. (1946)—b. 1901; ed. Rugby and Magdalene Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1939-45, lieut., Zanz. N.V.F.; ast. dist. comsnr., Zanz., 1924; secon. to D.O., 1933; dist. comsnr., Zanz., 1933; dist. offr., N. Rhod., 1936; dist. comsnr., Zanz., 1938; col. sec., Berm., 1945; ag. gov., Oct., 1945-May, 1946, and Mar.-Oct., 1949; dep. comsnr.-gen. (col. affrs.), S.E. Asia, 1950; gov. and c-in-c., Sey., 1953.
ADDISON, A. J.—b. 1922; ed. Msantsipim Sch., Achimota Coll. and Queen Mary Coll., London; prob. asst. engrnr., G.C., 1943; engrnr., 1948; regl. engrnr. (G.C. local service), 1955.
ADDISON, G. H.—b. 1911; ed. Kelsick Sch., Ambleside and Berlin Botanic Gdns.; mil. serv., 1941-46; curator, botanic gdns., S'pore, 1938.

ADDISON, J. S.—b. 1902; ed. Hillhead High Sch., Glasgow, and Edin. Univ.; mil. serv., 1941-42; ast. convstr., forests, F.M.S., 1925; senr. ast., 1935; secon. Ken., 1942-44; civ. affrs. and forest dept., B.M.A. (Mal.), 1944-46; convstr., forests, 1947.

ADDISON, W. R. L., M.C.—b. 1919; ed. Haileybury Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1939-46, maj.; cadet, Uganda, 1948; A.D.O., 1950; ast. sec., E.A.H.C., 1952; clk., cent. leg. assembly, 1951-55.

ADEMOLA, A. A.—b. 1906; ed. King's Coll., Lagos, and Camb. Univ.; barrister-at-law; mag., Nig., 1939; puisne judge, 1949; ch. justice, W. regn., 1955; mem. of comsns. for revision of cts. legn. (1948) and to enq. into Enugu disturbances (1949), Nig.

ADIE, J. J.—b. 1913; ed. Kingsland Grange Prep. Sch., Shrewsbury Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1940-42 (K.A.R. and O.E.T.A.); admin. offr., Zanz., 1937; ast. dist. comsnr., Pemba, 1938; Zanz., 1939; p.s. to Br. Res., 1939-40; ast. sec., 1942; senr. ast. sec. and p.s. to H.H. the Sultan, 1946; awarded Brill. Star of Zanz. (4th cl.), 1947; secon. C.O., 1949; sec., educ., labr. and lands, Ken., 1952; author of various articles on Zanzibar.

ADU, A. L., O.B.E. (1953).—b. 1914; ed. Presb. Church Schls., Boso and Anum, Achimota Coll. and Sch., and Queen's Coll., Camb.; master, Achimota Coll. and Sch., 1939; ast. dist. comsnr., G.C., 1942; comsnr. for Africaniation, 1950; admin. offr., cl. I, 1952; sec., external affrs., G.C. local service, 1955.
AGG, A. D.—b. 1912; ed. S. A. Collegiate Sch., Cape Town; mil. serv., 1939-40, 2nd lieut.; arch. asste., P.W.D., N. Rhod., 1938; ast. archt., 1945; archt., 1949; ast. D.P.W. (buildings), Nyasa, 1955.

AGIUS GILIBERT, C.—b. 1895; ed. Lyceum and Royal Univ. of Malta; a/c keeper, P.W.D. Malta, 1921; apptd. civ. serv. (admin.), 1923; ch. clk., 1942; electoral registr., 1945; prin. electoral offr. in addn., 1952.
AGOSTINI, C.—b. 1914; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin.; control offr., Trin., 1941; dep. aerodrome supt., 1946; dir. of civil aviation, 1947.

AGOSTINI, J. E.—b. 1906; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin.; cler. appts., govt. depts., Trin., 1924-44; senr. clk., acctnt-gen.'s dept., 1945; police (immig.), 1947; prin. offr., 1949; ast. ch. immig. offr., 1952; dep. ch. immig. offr., 1954.

AINLEY, A. J., M.C.—b. 1906; ed. St. Bees Sch. and Oxford Univ.; barrister-at-law; mil. serv., 1939-45, maj.; dist. mag., G.C., 1935; cr. coun., 1936; puisne judge, Uga., 1946; ch. just., E. Nig., 1954.
AITKEN, I. H.—b. 1919; ed. Greenock High Sch. and St. Andrews Univ.; tax assessor, Ken., 1944; senr. immig. offr., 1948; dep. prin. immig. offr., 1952.

AITKEN, Maj. R.—b. 1910; ed. Gregg Comm. Coll., Ipswich; mil. serv., 1939-46, major; H.M. Prison Service, 1936; secon. supt., prisons, B. Hond., 1946; resig.; N.Z. Prisons Serv., 1947; re-appointed supt., prisons, B. Hond., 1949; dep. dir. of prisons, J'ca, 1951; supt., prisons, B. Guiana, 1955.

AKERS, R. L.—b. 1912; ed. Solihull Sch. and Birmingham Univ.; mil. serv., 1941-42, 1944-46, capt.; ast. engrnr., drainage and irrig., F.M.S., 1936; senr. irrig. engrnr., S. Leone, 1943; engrnr., drainage and irrig., Mal., 1946; state engrnr., gr. B., 1949; chief construc. engrnr., drainage and irrig., Fed. of Mal., 1951.
AKESTER, H. W.—b. 1909; b'cast offr., G.C., 1945; tech. supt., 1953; chief tech. supt., b'casting, G.C. local serv., 1955.
AKIWUMI, A., M.B.E. (1951).—b. 1900; ed. Wesleyan Boys' Sch., Freetown, Queen's Coll., Taunton and Sch. of Metalliferous Mining, Cornwall; ass't inspr., mines, S.L., 1933 (regraded inspr., 1946).

AKWEI, E.—b. 1913; ed. Accra Govt. Boys' Sch., Achimota Sch. and Edin. Univ.; temp. clim. asst., G.C., 1941; temp. M.O., 1942; M.O., 1944; M.O.H., 1951; prin. M.O., 1953; chief M.O., G.C. local service, 1955.
ALBURY, K. H. E.—b. 1920; ed. Govt. High Sch., Nassau; clk., public treas'y, Bah., 1940; asst. audtr., 1950.

ALCOCK, A. E. S.—b. 1902; ed. County High Sch., Ilford, and Lond. Univ.; apptd. Ceylon, 1928; Tang., 1930; town engrnr., Kumasi, G.C., 1936; sec. and exec. offr., town and country planning bd., 1945; town plan. advr., 1952 (G.C. local service); co-author How to Plan your Village; pp. for U.N.O., etc., on stabilized soil building and planning.

ALCOCK, W. G.—b. 1907; ed. Oxford Prep. Sch., Glasgow Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; solr. (Scotland); asst. lands offr., Tang., 1935; lands offr. and registr-gen., Nyasa., 1939; off. assignee, S'pore, 1948; comsnr. of estate duties in addition.

ALDERTON, E. C.—b. 1908; ed. Sherborne Sch. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1939-45, lt.-col.; cadet, Nig., 1931; admin. offr., cl. II, 1950; cl.I, 1951; perm. sec., min. of internal affrs., E. regn., 1954.

ALDRIDGE, A. S., O.B.E. (1955), M.C., Cross of Valour (Poland).—b. 1918; ed. Durham Sch.; mil. serv., 1939-46, maj.; admin. offr., Cyp., 1946; cl. II, 1951; cl. I, 1953; senr. admin. offr., 1954; secon., C.O., 1955; secon., under-sec. (internal security), Cyprus, 1955.
ALEONG, R. V. S.—b. 1911; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trin., Faraday Hse. Elec. Engrng Coll., London; chief electric inspector, Trin., 1950.

ALEXANDER, A. L., Q.P.M. (1955), C.P.M. (1951).—b. 1907; ed. Wellingborough Sch.; Pal. police, 1931; sub-inspr., Trin., 1935; G.C., 1946; senr. asst. supt., 1948; asst. comsnr., 1949; dep. comsnr., G.C. local service, 1955.

ALEXANDER, J. B.—b. 1912; ed. Tiffin Boys' Sch., Kingston-on-Thames, Imp. Coll. of Science and Tech., London Univ. and California Inst. of Tech., U.S.A.; interned, 1942-45; geologist, Nyasa., 1935; F.M.S., 1939; prin. geol., 1950.

ALEXANDER, N. L.—b. 1907; ed. Clifton Coll., Bristol, and Oxford Univ.; interned, 1942-45; cadet, F.M.S., Chinese prot., 1931; cl. V, 1934, 3rd mag., Penang; D.O., Xmas I., 1936; asst. prot., Chinese, Perak, 1937; cl. IV, 1938; cl. III, 1943; dep. comsnr., labr., Pahang, Kelantan, 1946; cl. II, dep. comsnr., labr., Penang, 1947; cl. IC, asst. state sec. (B) Perak, 1950; sec., Chinese affrs., Selangor, 1951; cl. IB, 1952; D.O., Kinta, sec., Chinese affrs., Perak, 1954.

ALEXANDER, W. G., M.B.E.—b. 1918; ed. Sedbergh Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1940-45, major; admin. cadet, G. & E. Is. Col., 1946; co-op. soc. offr., 1947; registr., co-op. soc., Maur., 1951; comsnr., co-op. devl., Cyp., 1955; U.K. delegate, F.A.O./I.L.O. tech. mtg. on co-ops. in Asia and F.E., 1954; survey and Report on possibilities co-op. dev. and necessary co-op. legislation in G. & E. Is., 1947.
ALEXIS, F. C.—b. 1913; ed. Grenada Boys' Secondary Sch. and Royal Coll. of Surgeons, Edin.; dist. med. offr., St. V., 1935; supt. physician, col. hosp., Grenada, 1952.

ALFORD, R. E., C.M.G.—b. 1904; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; nav. serv., 1940-45; cadet, Nig., 1928; asst. dist. offr., 1931; dist. offr. (clk., exec. and leg. couns.), 1938; senr. dist. offr., 1946; fin. sec., Zanz., 1947; ch. sec., 1952.
ALI, Ayub, O.B.E. (1956), M.B.E.—b. 1907; ed. Govt. Indian Sch., Nairobi; clk., sec't, Ken., 1920; asst. estab. offr., 1944; estab. offr., 1948; asst. sec. (estab.), 1955.
ALLAN, C. H.—b. 1921; ed. Hamilton High Sch., Canterbury Univ. Coll., N.Z., and Camb. Univ.; mil. serv., 1942-44; cadet, B.S.I.P., 1945; cl. B., 1949; cl. A., 1955.

ALLAN, J. A.—b. 1910; ed. Glasgow Univ. and W. of Scotland Agric. Coll.; lectr., Barb., 1936; tech. offr., Nat. Inst. of Agric. Botany, Camb., 1944; supt., agric., Cyprus, 1945; pasture research offr., Ken., 1947; agric. offr., N. Rhod., 1949.

ALLAN, W., C.B.E. (1954), O.B.E.—b. 1904; ed. North Kelvinside Sch., Glasgow Univ. and I.C.T.A.; asst. research offr., agric. dept., N. Rhod., 1928; asst. dir., agric. and entom., 1938; dir., agric., Maur., 1948; Cyp., 1954.

ALLEN, C. P.—b. 1901; ed. Glasgow Univ.; anaesthetist, K. Lumpur, 1927; M.O. and surgeon, gen. hosp., Johore-Bahru, 1936; surgeon, gr. B, 1937; Perak, 1948; specialist surgeon, superscale gr. B, Selangor, 1951; gr. A, state surgeon, 1952.

ALLEN, C. P. S., M.V.O.—b. 1917; ed. Wanganui Collegiate Sch., N.Z., and Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1940-43, K.A.R.; admin. cadet, Uga., 1940; asst. dist. offr., 1942; asst. chief sec., 1953; perm. sec. for security and external relations, 1955.

ALLEN, D. M., M.B.E. (1949).—b. 1916; ed. Bloxham and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; cadet, admin., G.C., 1938; asst. dist. comsnr., 1939; dist. comsnr., 1945; admin. offr., cl. II, 1950; cl. I, 1952; perm. sec., G.C. local service, 1956.

ALLEN, E. C., O.B.E.—b. 1902; ed. Bradford Commercial Sch. and Sch. of Accountancy; clk., sec't., and reporter, leg. co., Nyasa, 1925; asst. treas., Tang., 1930; senr., 1940; sec'n., as treas., ch. of customs, British Som., 1940; fin. sec. and coll'r. of inc. tax, Basuto, 1945.

ALLEN, E. F.—b. 1914; ed. The Leys Sch. and Trinity Hall, Camb. and I.T.C.A.; mil. serv., 1941-45; agric. offr., F.M.S., 1937; B.M.A. (Mal.), 1943; agric. offr., 1946; agic. offr. (research), 1950; senr. agric. offr., 1950; (senr. agronomist), 1952; att. F.A.O. confs. Ceylon and Tokyo; cacao conf., London.

ALLEN, J. G. C., C.M.G. (1956).—b. 1904; ed. Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, Ecole Superieure de Commerce, Lausanne, and Munich Univ.; mil. serv., 1940-43, maj.; cadet, Nig., 1926; senr. dist. offr., 1945; cl. I., 1947; admin. offr., staff gr., 1953; E. regn., 1955; Br. comsnr., Anglo-French Cameroons Boundary Comsn., 1937-39; Nig. gov't rep. to Free French, Duala, 1940; i/c. mil. intell. and censorship, Nig., 1940-41; liaison offr. to Gen. Leclerc, 1942; mem., res. min's staff, Accra, 1943; labour offr., 1943-44; off. "goodwill" visit, Algeria and Morocco, 1949.
ALLEN, J. W. H.—b. 1913; ed. Regent St. Poly. and Downing Coll., Camb.; asst. audr., Nig., 1936; C.A.D., 1938; Fiji, 1943; senr. audr., Mal., 1948; dep. dir., fed. audit., Nig., 1954.

ALLEN, J. W. T.—b. 1904; ed. Westminster and St. John's Coll., Oxford; supt., educ., T.T., 1929; asst. dist. offr., 1937; dist. offr., 1942; political offr., Aden, 1947; dep. Br. agt., W. Aden Prot., 1948; D.O., Tang., 1953; senr., 1954.

ALLEN, T. H.—b. 1908; ed. Brockley Secondary Sch., London; interned, 1942-45; prob. inspr., police, S.S., 1929; inspr., 1932; senr., 1937; prob. asst. supt., police, S'pore, 1946; asst. supt., 1947; chief police offr., Brunei, 1948; supt., S'pore, 1952; supt., radio police, 1954.

ALLEYNE, K. H. C.—b. 1918; ed. Dominica Gram. Sch., Univ. Coll., London, and Queen's Coll., Oxford (Devonshire 2nd course); barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; registr., Dominica, 1943; mag., St. L., 1944; cr. atty., 1949; St. V., 1952; atty.-gen., Wind. Is., 1956; author of Memoir of constitutional development of St. Lucia, 1950.
ALLIN, P. W. M.—b. 1906; ed. Hilton Coll., Natal, and Rhodes Univ., Capetown; land survr., N. Rhod., 1930; senr. survr., 1945; dep. dir., surv. and land, 1951.
ALLSEBROOK, G. P.—b. 1918; ed. Stowe Sch., Kent Sch., Conn., U.S.A. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law; mil. serv., 1939-43, capt.; cadet, Tang., 1940; dist. offr., 1942.
ALVARES, R. E.—b. 1909; ed. St. Joseph's Coll., H.K. and H.K. Univ.; asst. pathologist, H.K., 1939; pathologist, 1951.

AMATO-GAUCI, F. E., M.B.E. (mil.) (1945)—b. 1917; ed. St. John Academy and Royal Univ. of Malta; mil. serv., 1939-45, maj.; civ. serv. (admin.), Malta, 1936; prin. offr., 1948; dir. of labr., 1949 (also ex off. regist., trade unions, and chmn., workmen's comp. bd., labr. bd., youth advsy. comtee., port workers' joint coun., port workers' bd.).

AMBROSE, J. W. D.—b. 1909; ed. Free Sch., Penang and Oxford Univ.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; asst. offl. assignee, S'pore, 1936; police mag. and asst. dist. judge, Malacca, 1940; registr., superior ct., 1945; dep. publ. prosecutor, N. Sembilan, 1946; senr. asst. registr., supreme cts. of Ipoh, Penang, K. Lumpur, 1947-52; pres., sessions ct., Penang, 1953; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., S'pore, 1955.

AMOROSO-CENTENO, A. E. B., M.B.E.—b. 1910; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Port-of-Spain; called to bar, Gray's Inn; clk., postal, treas. and acctnt.-gen's depts., Trin., 1928-42; assessor, inl. rev. dept., 1943; sec., 1950; dep. comsnr., 1952.
ANDERSEN, V. J., M.B.E. (1955).—b. 1919; ed. Napier High Sch. and Auckland Univ. Coll., N.Z.; mil. serv., 1940-46, lieut.; cadet, B.S.I.P., 1947; cl. B, 1950; cl. A, 1954.

ANDERSON, A. H. G.—b. 1901; ed. privately; p.s. to gov., B. Hond., 1927; asst. contrlr., food and prices, 1939; contrlr., 1940; dist. comsnr., 1942; asst. sec. and archaeol. comsnr., 1953; author of Brief Sketch of British Honduras; British Honduras and articles on archaeology and history.

ANDERSON, A. R., O.B.E. (1955), Q.P.M. (1953), C.P.M. (1948).—b. 1910; ed. Dreghorn Castle Sch. and Bedford Sch.; interned, 1942-45; prob., S.S., 1929; asst. supt., police, 1934; o/c naval base police, S'pore, 1941; supt., police, S'pore, 1948; comdt., police training sch., 1949; asst. comsnr., police, 1952.
ANDERSON, D. A.—b. 1920; ed. Bathgate Academy and Edin. Univ.; cadet, G.C., 1941; dist. comsnr., admin. offr., cl. II., 1951; cl. I(G.C. local service), 1955.
ANDERSON, E. A.—b. 1908; ed. Diocesan High Sch., Belize and Toronto Univ.; apprentice surveyor, B. Hond., 1929; govt. surveyor, 1932; senr. surveyor, 1953.

ANDERSON, R. E.—b. 1903; ed. Guardbridge Sch., Madras Coll., and St. Andrews Univ.; health offr., Mal., 1930; admin. M.O., super-scale, gr. B, 1948; gr. A, 1950; asst. D.M.S., Mal., 1952; D.M.S., 1953.

ANDERSON, Lieut.-Col. S. A., C.P.M.—b. 1916; ed. Jamaica, Metrop. Police Training Course, London, and Fingerprint Course and Criminal Record Office Course, New Scotland Yard; mil. serv., 1939-45; J'ca constab., 1936; col. police serv., J'ca, 1951; supt., police, St. V., 1954.

ANDERSON, T. F., O.B.E. (mil.)—b. 1901; ed. Rugby, Trinity Hall, Camb., and Univ. Coll. Hosp.; mil. serv., 1939-46, col. (desps.); med. offr., Ken., 1928; D.M.S., Som., 1946; D.D.M.S., Ken., 1949; D.M.S., 1949; author of various papers on medicine and health in E. Africa.
ANDERSON, Capt. V.—b. 1907; ed. Robt. Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; master mariner; Tang. and E.A. Rys. and harbours, 1941; marine supt., G.C., 1951. (G.C. local service).
ANDERTON, R. S.—b. 1905; ed. Municipal Coll., Portsmouth and Battersea Poly., London; prin., tech. inst., Kingston, J'ca., 1947; Yaba, Nig., 1949; advr. on tech. educ., S'pore, 1954.
ANDRE, J. A. H., M.B.E.—b. 1898; ed. Primary Sch., Maur., Royal Coll., Maur., and L.S.H.T.M.; govt. med. offr., 1924; D.D.M.S., Maur., 1951.
ANDREW-BLAMEY, W. H. J.—b. 1918; ed. Probus Sch., Truro; mil. serv., 1939-46; col. audit serv., Nig., 1946; St. H., 1948; Ken., 1952; col. treas. and collr., cust., St. H., 1952; Uga., 1955.

ANGELO-THOMSON, W. T.—b. 1908; ed. Dulwich Coll., Mercers' Sch., and Law Society Sch. of Law; solicitor, Eng.; mil. serv., 1939, W.O. staff, 1941-53; H.M. land registry, 1937-38; asst. prosecutor (ch. legal off.) L.P.T.B., 1938; registr.-gen. and registr., sup. ct., Berm., 1954.

ANGELONI, R., M.B.E.—b. 1920; ed. Lycée Lakanal, France, Sevenoaks Sch. and St. Catharine's Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1941-42; cadet, Nig., 1942; cl. III, 1950; cl. "B", W. Pac., 1955; second., admin. offr., N. Heb., 1955.

ANGUS, H. A., M.B.E.—b. 1908; ed Central British Sch., H.K.; clk., H.K., 1924; senr. cler. and acctg. offr., 1927; cl. I, 1946; senr. exec. offr., cl. I, 1948; asst. dir., commerce and industry, 1951; dir., 1953.
ANGUS, J. R.—b. 1912; ed. Malvern Coll., Glas. High Sch. and Edin. Univ.; mil. serv., 1940-41, lt.; asst. consvr., forests, Ken., 1940-50; consvr., forests, Fiji, 1951.

ANGUS, S.—b. 1913; ed. Batley Sch. and Oxford Univ.; mil. serv., 1942-46; cadet, S.S. and F.M.S., 1936; stamp offr., S'pore, 1938; cl. V, asst. D.O., Kinta, 1939; asst. advr., Pontian, 1942; cl. IV, 1943; D.O., K. Pilah, 1946; cl. III, 1947; cl. IC, 1952; comsnr., lands and mines, Treng., 1954.
